key_insight: PTFE spray coating, especially in multi-coat form, improves thickness, surface finish, and protection across multiple substrates, with Inconel 600 performing best overall.
engineering_relevance: The study supports PTFE-coated substrates for industrial parts needing better corrosion resistance, durability, and lower maintenance in high-temperature or cost-sensitive applications.
method: Surface-prepared mild steel, aluminum alloy, and Inconel 600 samples were coated using spray-deposited PTFE.
Single-coat and multi-coat PTFE layers were cured at 380–410 °C.
Thickness, tensile strength, hardness, roughness, microstructure, and salt-spray corrosion were evaluated using ASTM/IS-based tests.
evidence_signal: Test results showed multi-coat PTFE produced thicker coatings, all substrates gained smoother surfaces, and Inconel 600 had the strongest mechanical and finish performance.
watch_item: Interpret the corrosion claims cautiously because the salt-spray test duration was short and wear testing was not included.
paper_title: Investigation of Mechanical Properties of Various Substrate Materials using Polytetrafluoroethylene Coating Technique
